segments.text: And it came to pass after this, that Absalom prepared him chariots and horses, and fifty men to run before him.
And Absalom rose up early, and stood beside the way of the gate: and it was so, that when any man that had a controversy came to the king for judgment, then Absalom called unto him,
Of what city art thou?
segments.speaker: Absalom

segments.text: Thy servant is of one of the tribes of Israel.
segments.speaker: Unknown

segments.text: See, thy matters are good and right; but there is no man deputed of the king to hear thee.
segments.speaker: Absalom

footnotes: there is no man deputed: or, none will hear thee from the king downward

segments.text: Oh that I were made judge in the land, that every man which hath any suit or cause might come unto me, and I would do him justice!
segments.speaker: Absalom

segments.text: And it was so, that when any man came nigh to him to do him obeisance, he put forth his hand, and took him, and kissed him.
And on this manner did Absalom to all Israel that came to the king for judgment: so Absalom stole the hearts of the men of Israel.
I pray thee, let me go and pay my vow, which I have vowed unto the LORD, in Hebron.
segments.speaker: Absalom

segments.text: For thy servant vowed a vow while I abode at Geshur in Syria, saying, If the LORD shall bring me again indeed to Jerusalem, then I will serve the LORD.
segments.speaker: Absalom

segments.text: Go in peace.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: So he arose, and went to Hebron.
As soon as ye hear the sound of the trumpet, then ye shall say, Absalom reigneth in Hebron.
segments.speaker: Absalom

segments.text: And there came a messenger to David, saying, The hearts of the men of Israel are after Absalom.
Arise, and let us flee; for we shall not else escape from Absalom: make speed to depart, lest he overtake us suddenly, and bring evil upon us, and smite the city with the edge of the sword.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: Wherefore goest thou also with us? return to thy place, and abide with the king: for thou art a stranger, and also an exile.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: As the LORD liveth, and as my lord the king liveth, surely in what place my lord the king shall be, whether in death or life, even there also will thy servant be.
segments.speaker: Ittai

segments.text: Carry back the ark of God into the city: if I shall find favour in the eyes of the LORD, he will bring me again, and shew me both it, and his habitation:
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: O LORD, I pray thee, turn the counsel of Ahithophel into foolishness.
segments.speaker: David

segments.text: I will be thy servant, O king; as I have been thy father's servant hitherto, so will I now also be thy servant: then mayest thou for me defeat the counsel of Ahithophel.
segments.speaker: David
